Second Sunday of Easter, April 12th at 11 AM

sources.name: scn_Welcome and Acknowledgment of the Land

items.name: txt_Welcome and Acknowledgment of the Land

sources.name: txt_Welcome and Acknowledgment of the Land

settings.text:
Welcome and Acknowledgment of the Land 
 Trinity Lutheran Church acknowledges that 
 we live and worship on the traditional 
 territories of the Indigenous peoples of 
 the Treaty 6 region and the Métis Nation 
 of Alberta, Region 4. Together we call 
 upon all of our collective, honoured 
 traditions and spirits to work in 
 continuing right relationship that we may 
 create a more vibrant, healthy community 
 for today and future generations. 
 P: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, 
 the love of God, and the communion of the 
 Holy Spirit be with you all. 
 C: And also with you. 
 P: God of new life and new hope 
 C: We come to the empty tomb, and we see 
 our own death and our own tomb. We 
 remember how we have treated other 
 people: members of our family, friends 
 and neighbours. We come to the empty tomb 
 to search within ourselves that which we 
 cannot escape: the truth of who we are, a 
 people caught up in the pain of our own 
 brokenness, loneliness and a frustration 
 of what we would be but are not. Our 
 wounds are deep and we look to you for 
 hope. 
 P: Creator of Life 
 C: In raising Christ Jesus up beyond the 
 chains of death, You opened for us the 
 way to eternal and abundant life. May our 
 alleluias float high above the ceiling of 
 what we think we know and transcend to 
 the heaven of what we believe— and thus 
 live. Raise us up! Renew our lives! 
 Resurrect our dreams! Through Jesus 
 Christ our Living Savior, Amen. 
 C: Thanks be to God 
 C: Thanks be to God 
 P: The Gospel according to John 20:19-31 
 C: Glory to you, O Lord 
 P: This is the gospel of the Lord 
 C: Praise to you, O Christ. 
 P: God, in your mercy 
 C: Hear our prayer 
 P: The Lord be with you 
 C: And also with you. 
 P: Lift up your hearts. 
 C: We lift them to the Lord. 
 P: Let us give our thanks and praise to 
 the Lord our God. 
 C: It is right to give our thanks and 
 praise. 
 P: We have testified that this mystery of 
 salvation found in Jesus Christ is for 
 all, when we say . . . 
 C: Christ has died, Christ is risen, 
 Christ will come again.

Dismissal 
 P: God is, love does C: In the footsteps 
 of grace we become, and we follow!
